LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILED TO NEGATE THE FACTS OF EXPOSURE SET FORTH IN PLAINTIFFS ANSWERS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF ANTONIO HERNANDEZ AND WILLIS HAMILTON IS NOT NEGATED; NOR ARE THE FACTS RELATED TO UCC'S KNOWLEDGE, WHICH IS THE BASIS FOR THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN , CSR # 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT W.F. HAYWARD COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DECEDENT'S SON, ANTONIO HERNANDEZ, JR. TESTIFIED IN HIS DEPOSITION THAT HE SAW DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES ON JOB SITES HE WORKED WITH DECEDENT USING ASBESTOS RELATED MATERIALS WHILE DOING LATHING, PLASTERING AND TAPING WORK. HE COULD IDENTIFY THEM AS DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES BY THEIR TRUCKS, SCAFFOLDING AND BY TALKING TO THEM. EVEN IF THE BURDEN HAS SHIFTED, THERE WOULD BE A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES WERE ON THE SAME JOB SITE AS DECEDENT USING ASBESTOS MATERIALS. SEE ABOVE. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: PATRICK HEARD, CSR # 11055
